meta_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve No Portfolio (Tutorial)
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about a lack of portfolio. Learn how to use Git, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build and ship high-value SaaS MVPs at lightning speed.
content.answer_block: A lack of portfolio is a vanity problem; clients and investors care about functional, revenue-generating products. By leveraging Git-integrated AI tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can architect, code, and deploy production-ready SaaS MVPs in days, effectively turning your development process into a living, verifiable portfolio of real-world results.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the silent killer of ambition. For a founder, the 'no portfolio' trap is a psychological barrier that obscures the truth: the market doesn't care about your past work; it cares about the utility of your current solution. You don't need a decade of agency work to prove your worth—you need a shipping cadence that others can't match.

We are entering the era of the 'AI-Augmented Founder,' where the barrier to entry isn't technical proficiency, but the ability to translate vision into code. By integrating Git with AI-native workflows, you aren't just writing code; you are building a repository of value that serves as both your product and your ultimate proof of skill.

steps.title: Define the Value-First MVP
steps.description: Before touching code, define a single, high-value feature that solves a specific pain point. This keeps your Git history focused and your product lean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Define a lean MVP architecture for a SaaS that solves [Insert Problem]. Focus on modularity and rapid deployment. List the core Git repository structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude -p 'Initialize a new project structure following the proposed modular architecture.'

steps.title: Bootstrapping with Version Control
steps.description: Initialize your repository early. Using Git from day one ensures every iteration is tracked and verifiable for your future portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a .gitignore for a modern SaaS stack and provide the bash commands to initialize a clean, professional Git repository.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git add . && git commit -m 'feat: initial project structure'

steps.title: AI-Assisted Feature Engineering
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write your core logic. Focus on writing clean, commented code that demonstrates your engineering standard.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core business logic for [Feature Name] using clean, idiomatic code. Include error handling and unit tests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Implement the business logic for the core feature, ensuring 90% test coverage.'

steps.title: Automating Documentation and Commits
steps.description: A great portfolio isn't just code; it's clear communication. Use AI to generate semantic commit messages that explain the 'why' behind the 'what'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a git commit message template that follows conventional commits, focusing on business value delivered.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Generate a semantic commit message for the recent changes, highlighting the user benefit.'

steps.title: Deploying for Public Validation
steps.description: Your portfolio is only as good as its live availability. Connect your Git repo to a deployment pipeline to prove your product works in the wild.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a deployment configuration for Vercel/Fly.io that links to my GitHub repo for automated CI/CD.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Create a production-ready CI/CD configuration file for automated deployment on push.'

pro_tips: Treat your Git history as a marketing asset; meaningful commit messages show potential investors your development maturity.
Always commit your 'Why' in the README.md; founders who articulate the business logic win more than those who just write code.
Use branching strategies (Gitflow) even for solo projects; it demonstrates you understand professional software engineering best practices.
Automate your testing suite; an MVP with zero bugs is more impressive than a complex app with a broken user experience.
Keep your PRs small and focused; it makes it easier for others to review your code and see your progress over time.
resources: Git Best Practices for Founders: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, share the link in public forums and invite feedback. Use the feedback loop to iterate using the same AI-Git workflow, effectively building your reputation in real-time.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be an expert. You have the tools, the stack, and the roadmap. Every commit is a brick in the foundation of your future company. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
